title: atropa bella-donna subsp. baetica
intro: Atropa bella-donna subsp. baetica, also known as the Baetic Belladonna, is a subspecies of the deadly nightshade plant (Atropa bella-donna) native to southern Spain and northwestern Africa. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include antispasmodic, anti-inflammatory, and analgesic effects. Its health benefits include relief from pain, inflammation, and muscle spasms, as well as potential antiviral and antibacterial properties. Atropa bella-donna subsp. baetica can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and is also used in homeopathic remedies.
